programmes.show_description: K Is For Kill Part 2: Tiger By The Tail: With many of the assailants accounted for, the team are faced with tracking down the surviving agents before they start World War III. Part 2 of 2.
Love All: Steed and Tara investigate an unlikely suspect who is suddenly attracting the romantic affections of bureaucrats who are too keen to share their top secret information with her.
Mulcahy's War: Frank claims a patient shot himself to avoid combat and calls Father Mulcahy. Troubled by his lack of experience at the front, he accompanies Radar to an aid station there.
The Korean Surgeon: Instead of turning him in as a prisoner of war, Hawkeye and B.J. pull some strings to get a wounded North Korean surgeon a spot on the staff.
Hawkeye Get Your Gun: Hawkeye and Potter provide assistance at a Korean clinic, and run into an enemy attack on the way home. Klinger poses as a Gypsy to help get his discharge papers.
Julie: Henry's teenage daughter Julie comes to stay for a couple of days, but tensions soon mount, and Henry and Matthew soon question whether three is a crowd.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Match Made In Heaven: Jonathan's friend Scotty, a quadriplegic, wonders if his relationship with a woman he likes can succeed.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Banker And Bum: Melvyn, a cold-hearted tycoon swaps places with Willie, a kind-hearted homeless man to see what it would be like if they each lived different lives.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 1: Jonathan steps in to help Deke, a resentful young athlete whose legs are amputated below the knee following a tragic motorcycle accident.
One Fresh Batch Of Lemonade: Part 2: Jonathan sets out to restore Deke's faith in himself with the help of a young gymnast.
Troublemaker: When Miss Beadle is deemed unable to control some of the older boys in her class, Mrs Oleson convinces the school board to replace her with a firmer, male hand.
The Long Road Home: Charles and Mr Edwards take a dangerous job handling highly explosive liquid dynamite.
For My Lady: Caroline and the girls think the worst when Charles begins to behave mysteriously and lies about the amount of time he has spent doing carpentry with an attractive neighbour.
The Seashore: The Waltons go on vacation to the Baldwins' beach cottage, where they meet a mysterious English girl. Ben tries to enjoy the carefree life when he is left home alone.
The Volunteer: John and Olivia must accept that Erin is growing up when G.W. Haines proposes marriage.
Moving Out: Emma has had enough of her father's strange behaviour towards Niamh, but what will Ballykissangel's gossips think of her moving in with Danny?
The Great Leap Forward: The residents of Ormston welcome a new face. Donald and Deborah set up a mobile blood bank to try and find a match for teenage haemophiliac Abel Marl.
Dr Robert Bramwell's attempts to match his daughter Eleanor with an old army friend seem doomed to failure. Could a boxer with a head injury bring Eleanor and Major Hyde together in the end?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Golden Thread: Rumpole finds himself embroiled in the world of politics when he flies to Africa to defend a former pupil who has been charged with murder.
38 Across: Flummoxed by a crossword puzzle, Hawkeye persuades his old friend, Tippy Brook - an expert at puzzles, to come to the camp.
Ping Pong: The 4077th hosts a traditional Korean wedding, much to Frank's objections. Colonel Potter runs into an old friend who has put his troops in jeopardy to receive a promotion.
End Run: A former football player is distraught by the resulting surgery after a severe leg injury. Frank goes behind Colonel Potter's back to set up a boxing match between Klinger and Zale.
Lionel is determined to stop Jacko's relationship with his daughter Leslie, but a chance meeting between Jacko and brother Reg may do the job for him.
Lionel's daughter Leslie is on a forced separation from Jacko in America. Meanwhile, after a spell in hospital, it appears that Jacko's old charm is not working with the ladies.
Jacko's in turmoil, having received both a declaration of love from Lesley and a perceived come-on from Veronica - her mother! Lionel's preoccupied with a loan for the business.
Complex: The Avengers travel to Canada on a mission to discover the identity of a member of the KGB. Meanwhile, Purdey finds herself in danger when she is trapped inside a building.
Take Me To Your Leader: Steed and Tara are tasked with tracking the movements of a briefcase that is being passed around between enemy agents. Will the case expose a national security risk?
38 Across: Flummoxed by a crossword puzzle, Hawkeye persuades his old friend, Tippy Brook - an expert at puzzles, to come to the camp.
Ping Pong: The 4077th hosts a traditional Korean wedding, much to Frank's objections. Colonel Potter runs into an old friend who has put his troops in jeopardy to receive a promotion.
End Run: A former football player is distraught by the resulting surgery after a severe leg injury. Frank goes behind Colonel Potter's back to set up a boxing match between Klinger and Zale.
Human Interest: Henry and Matthew find themselves needing to find a new housekeeper when Enid wins money on the premium bonds. Will they be able to agree on a suitable replacement?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Brightest Star: Jonathan and Mark chasten Laurie, a young actress, whose fame is turning her into a monster, fracturing her family and robbing her of her childhood.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
An Investment In Caring: Jonathan must get Helen's neighbours to join together and help save their homes from being seized by corporate developers.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Discover Shop Unlimited, where you'll find great value products, clever home essentials, inspiring craft ideas and exciting finds you didn't know you needed. Shop anytime at www.shopunlimitedlive.com.

Discover Shop Unlimited, where you'll find great value products, clever home essentials, inspiring craft ideas and exciting finds you didn't know you needed. Shop anytime at www.shopunlimitedlive.com.
A Divine Madness: Mr Crook, convinced he is King Arthur, is in danger of being declared incompetent by his son. Can Jonathan work a reconciliation?
Catch A Falling Star: Jonathan and Mark have been hired to look after the kids of a movie star who never has time to spend with his family.
Centennial: Walnut Grove prepares to celebrate the nation's centennial, but the party is threatened with cancellation when their taxes go up.
Soldier's Return: Coming home after 12 years of performing with a busy Philadelphia orchestra, Mrs. Whipple's troubled son returns to Walnut Grove.
Going Home: The Ingalls farm and livelihood lies in tatters when a devastating tornado sweeps through Walnut Grove.
The Grandchild: Part 1: Mary Ellen is worried that bad omens will threaten the health of her unborn child. Part 1 of 2.
The Grandchild: Part 2: Mary Ellen is worried that bad omens will threaten the health of her unborn child. Part 2 of 2.
Eureka: The lure of a goldmine leads Quigley into a race against time. He isn't the only one who believes they've struck gold, as a little help from Orla goes a long way with Conor.
Flying Home: Helen returns to Ormston to join the villagers in paying their last respects to Tom. Meanwhile, Henry suggests that Deborah and Donald sell the cottage hospital to the NHS.
Eleanor and Robert are making slow progress on the underground railway when the train suddenly leaves the rails and crashes into the side of the tunnel.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old Boy Net: Alongside his pupil Fiona Allways, Rumpole defends a couple charged with blackmail and with running an immoral house. But who are the couple trying to protect?
Hanky Panky: BJ spends the night with a nurse after consoling her about her troubled marriage. Meanwhile Margaret is upset because she hasn't received a letter from her fiancé recently.
Hepatitis: Hawkeye's back pains seem minimal compared to Father Mulcahy, who has arthritis and is jaundiced under the tongue. Hawkeye suspects hepatitis and has to inoculate the staff.
The General's Practitioner: In the midst of Hawkeye being considered for the position of a general's personal physician, Radar becomes a surrogate father to a Korean woman and her baby.
...Likely Lads?: End of an Era: The day of the wedding dawns, and the women are racing around getting ready for the big event. Meanwhile, Terry has his hands full looking after Bob.
...Likely Lads?: Absent Friends: Bob and Thelma are on a Norwegian honeymoon, far away from the influence of Terry - or so they think.
...Likely Lads?: Heart to Heart: Bob comes back from his honeymoon to find that Terry is off his beer and is going out with Thelma's sister Susan. Is Terry in love?
Sleeping Partners: Robin Tripp, now a fully qualified, unemployed chef, harbours ambitions of opening his own bistro...for people who appreciate high prices.
